requirements.note: git rename from ci/data/pipelines.js to github-actions/data/pipelines.js confirmed. Import paths unchanged (relative paths still valid). Comment updated from CI/CD to GitHub Actions.
ci/preset.json: scan block removed. github-actions/preset.json: scan.include added with .github/workflows/**/*.yml and .yaml patterns.
Both en/ja ci_cd.md templates changed from {{data}} to {{text}} with prompt parameters. {%block%} directives retained. Each block (pipelines, jobs, env) now uses {{text}}.
New en/ja ci_cd.md created under github-actions/templates/ with {%extends%} and {{data("github-actions.pipelines.*")}} overrides for all three blocks.

requirements.desc: aliases削除: ci から github-actions を削除
移行手順明示: type ci → github-actions
requirements.note: Migration instructions documented in spec.md Impact on Existing Features section and draft.md.

requirements.desc: テストリネーム: ci-pipelines → github-actions-pipelines
requirements.note: File renamed from ci-pipelines.test.js to github-actions-pipelines.test.js. Import paths updated to github-actions/data/pipelines.js. Describe labels updated.
Test file changes look correct but the diff itself does not contain evidence of npm test execution results. issue-log.json notes tests passed with 0 failures but this is indirect evidence.

unplanned.change: New draft document (part of SDD workflow, not a code change)
Issue log for gate-impl false positive
Code review results documenting 7 findings (4 approved, 3 rejected) — these are broader refactoring suggestions unrelated to this spec's scope
Spec verification test covering all requirements — useful but not explicitly required by the spec
JSDoc comment changed from 'CI/CD pipelines' to 'GitHub Actions pipelines' — minor but not explicitly required

summary.notes: All 8 requirements are satisfied in the diff. The core implementation (DataSource move, scan config move, template abstraction, template inheritance, aliases removal, test rename) is complete and correct. The migration instructions are documented in spec artifacts. Test execution evidence is indirect (issue-log.json reference) rather than direct npm test output in the diff. The unplanned changes are all SDD workflow artifacts (specs/, review, verification tests) which are expected process outputs, not unintended code changes. The only actual code change not covered by requirements is the minor JSDoc comment update in pipelines.js.
